title: zea mexicana
intro: Zea mexicana, also known as Mexican corn or teosinte, is an ancient wild grass that is a precursor to modern maize (corn) and belongs to the Poaceae family. Traditionally, Zea mexicana has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and infections. The health benefits of Zea mexicana include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood sugar levels, and improve digestion. Zea mexicana can be prepared as a tea or capsule, and its extracts can be used in herbal preparations, such as tinctures and salves, to harness its medicinal properties.
